tasks_completed: piece 1: queue.py empty/whitespace --lane coerces to no-filter (None) instead of literal "" key; enumerate_dispatchable line ~120 + main() CLI coercion widened to whitespace; docstring updated. Added TestEmptyLaneIsNoFilter (4 cases). 166 pass; CLI smoke confirms enumerate + fingerprint byte-for-byte identical between --lane "" and no --lane.

piece 2: templates/prompts/queen.md — both queue.py selection invocations (assess step ~line 21, dispatch step ~line 39) now pass --lane "$LOOP_LANE"; added a one-line note explaining empty LOOP_LANE = no filter. Commit 8ca0d54. Proved the selection path is lane-scoped at the shell-expansion level: `LOOP_LANE=""; queue.py . --lane "$LOOP_LANE"` output is byte-for-byte IDENTICAL (diff empty) to legacy `queue.py .`. 166 pass.

piece 3: bin/loop cmd_install_service parses optional --lane <name> (positional interval preserved); templates/com.scaviefae.simpleloop.plist gained two own-line placeholders {{LOOP_LANE_KEY}}/{{LOOP_LANE_VAL}}. Lane set -> single-line sed subst adds LOOP_LANE to EnvironmentVariables; lane absent -> both placeholder lines deleted, plist byte-for-byte stock. Commit 2de2404. Verified: no-lane tail diffs empty vs pre-change template + 0 LOOP_LANE keys; with-lane passes plutil, LOOP_LANE=remote-queens. 166 pass.

closing (iter 4): wrote closeout.md (three pieces 1a5f0bc/8ca0d54/2de2404, per-criterion pass table, single-daemon parity proofs at all three seams, lessons) + review.md (gate runbook for Human-gate: review, links closeout for what-shipped, 5-min parity-check runbook, recommend approve). Commit 5e9e747. 166 pass, tree clean. Status -> complete.
learnings: Piece 1 was largely pre-existing: main() already coerced exact --lane "" to None (queue.py:294-297). The real gap was (a) enumerate_dispatchable(lane="") called directly still fail-closed via lane_key="", and (b) whitespace-only lanes ('   ') were not coerced anywhere. Both fixed.

queue_fingerprint branches on `lane is None` — so keeping main()'s empty→None coercion is load-bearing for byte-for-byte fingerprint parity, not just enumerate. Verified: fingerprint identical between --lane "" and no --lane (ae018aafb9938ade).

Wiring map for remaining pieces: queen.md does selection via `queue.py .` (lines ~21,39, NO --lane today). daemon.sh parses --lane / LOOP_LANE env at lines 40-57 and builds _LANE_OPT, exports LOOP_LANE at 50-51. plist template EnvironmentVariables block is at templates/com.scaviefae.simpleloop.plist:37-45; bin/loop interpolates it via sed at bin/loop:1972-1981 (ProgramArguments carry only project_dir + interval, no lane).
No .loop/config.sh / VERIFY_CMD in this worktree. Verify manually with: python3 -m pytest lib/tests/ -q.
Human-gate: review — closing iteration must produce both closeout.md AND review.md in wiki/briefs/cards/brief-152-queen-lane-wiring/.

Piece 2 (iter 2): queen.md is a prompt template, not pytest-testable directly. Verified the lane-scoping at the seam it actually runs through — shell expansion of `--lane "$LOOP_LANE"`. Empty LOOP_LANE → no filter (piece 1 coercion), diff vs legacy `queue.py .` empty. daemon.sh:50-51 exports LOOP_LANE; _LANE_OPT (daemon.sh:55-56) is for the daemon's own fingerprint/dedup path, separate from the queen's selection invocation that piece 2 just wired.

Piece 3 (iter 3): plist conditional injection done WITHOUT multi-line sed. Two placeholders each alone on a line — no-lane path is `/{{...}}/d` (line-delete -> byte-for-byte stock parity, the load-bearing guard); lane path is single-line `s|...|...|` (| delimiter avoids escaping the </key> slashes; lane name sed-escaped for |&). Daemon needs NO ProgramArguments change — it reads LOOP_LANE from env (daemon.sh:50), so plist EnvironmentVariables is the only wiring point. cmd_install_service now parses --lane/--lane= and trims whitespace (whitespace-only lane stays no-filter, consistent with piece 1). Closing iteration only needs closeout.md + review.md; no code left.

Closing (iter 4): brief COMPLETE. Both artifacts in wiki/briefs/cards/brief-152-queen-lane-wiring/. closeout.md is the authoritative what-shipped; review.md (Human-gate: review) links it rather than duplicating, per the worker contract. review.md recommends APPROVE — merge gated on Mattie (Auto-merge: false).
